cues.label: Fill to k
cues.headline: Heap not full yet
cues.narration: The first k values just go in; nothing is evicted until the cap is reached.

cues.label: Overflow evicts the smallest
cues.headline: Pop the weakest survivor
cues.narration: When a new value pushes the heap past k, pop the root — the smallest candidate.

cues.label: Root is the k-th largest
cues.headline: Smallest of the winners
cues.narration: The min-heap's root is the smallest of the k largest — exactly the k-th largest.

cues.label: Why min not max
cues.headline: Evict the weakest, not the strongest
cues.narration: A max-heap would guard the biggest; you want the smallest winner on the chopping block.

cues.label: Cost and memory
cues.headline: n log k, O(k) space
cues.narration: Each operation touches a size-k heap, so the pass is n log k using only k slots.

cues.label: The trap
cues.headline: Do not grab a max-heap
cues.narration: Reaching for a max-heap on instinct evicts winners and returns a wrong answer.
